value.prior_session_context: Tuesday 2026-06-23 saw a global technology and semiconductor selloff; the KOSPI plunged roughly 10%, its sharpest one-day drop since March, and the Nikkei also fell. June 24 was the first stabilization session after that rout.

text: Wednesday, June 24, 2026 was a regular trading day for the referenced Asia-Pacific cash-equity markets. The session followed a bruising Tuesday June 23, when a global technology and semiconductor selloff sent the KOSPI down roughly 10% in its sharpest one-day drop since March and pushed the Nikkei lower as well. All referenced markets operated on their normal Wednesday schedules; there were no market holidays.

nikkei_225.basis: WSJ market data and MarketWatch/FactSet close; previous close 69,788.38

kospi.basis: Yonhap close bulletin; MarketWatch corroborates rounded +3.3%

hang_seng.basis: MarketWatch/FactSet close; Yahoo Finance At close 4:08 PM GMT+8

value.basis_note: MSCI Asia ex-Japan gained 0.04% per Reuters

calculation.method: Nikkei: WSJ market data shows 69,174.97 minus previous close 69,788.38 equals -613.41 points (-0.88%); KOSPI: Yonhap gives +267.18 points to 8,471.02 (+3.26%); Hang Seng: MarketWatch/Yahoo show +75.90 to 23,412.18 (+0.33%); Shanghai Composite and ASX 200 per MarketWatch/FactSet rounded percents

text: Asia-Pacific equity markets closed mixed on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, after a global technology selloff the prior session. The Nikkei 225 fell 613.41 points (-0.88%) to 69,174.97, the KOSPI surged 267.18 points (+3.26%) to 8,471.02, the Hang Seng rose 75.90 points (+0.33%) to 23,412.18, and the Shanghai Composite added 0.11% to 4,110.81. MarketWatch also put the S&P/ASX 200 up 0.24% to 8,808.40 and Singapore's FTSE Straits Times up 0.20% to 5,216.44. MSCI's index of Asian equities outside Japan rose 0.04%.

value.prior_session_context: Tuesday June 23 KOSPI plunge was the sharpest one-day drop since March, driven by global semiconductor and AI-valuation selloff
value.intraday_volatility: KOSPI opened higher, briefly fell into negative territory on heavy foreign selling, and dropped close to 8,000 before reversing course on retail and institutional buying

calculation.method: Yonhap close bulletin gives KOSPI at 8,471.02 up 267.18 points (+3.26%); Reuters independently confirms the +3.26% figure and notes the prior session 10% plunge; trade volume was 413.4 million shares worth 53.78 trillion won

text: South Korea's KOSPI rebounded 267.18 points, or 3.26%, to close at 8,471.02 on Wednesday, June 24, after choppy trading. The rebound followed a roughly 10% crash on Tuesday, June 23, the market's sharpest one-day drop since March. Foreign investors sold a net 4.66 trillion won, but retail and institutional buyers purchased 2.63 trillion and 1.92 trillion won respectively. Samsung Electronics jumped 9.84% and SK hynix rose 0.98%. The Korean won weakened to 1,541.8 per dollar.

value.yen_context: Yen hovered near 40-year lows, keeping markets on edge over potential currency intervention

calculation.method: WSJ market data shows Nikkei 225 at 69,174.97, down 613.41 points (-0.88%) from previous close of 69,788.38; MarketWatch independently confirms -0.88% to 69,174.97

text: Japan's Nikkei 225 fell for a second consecutive day on Wednesday, June 24, closing down 613.41 points or 0.88% at 69,174.97. The index traded in a wide 68,461.10 to 70,218.71 intraday range, reflecting volatile conditions. The yen traded around 161.81 per dollar, near 40-year lows, keeping markets on edge over potential intervention. MarketWatch noted the decline was part of a mixed regional pattern where Japanese shares lagged while Hong Kong and Seoul rose.

hang_seng.basis: MarketWatch/FactSet and Yahoo Finance close at 4:08 PM GMT+8

value.hang_seng_leaders: MarketWatch listed Semicon Mfg Intl +8.93%, WuXi AppTec +8.33%, and Wuxi Biologics +4.17% among top Hang Seng gainers

market_session: Hong Kong and Shanghai regular cash-equity closes, Wednesday 2026-06-24

calculation.method: MarketWatch/FactSet close: Hang Seng at 23,412.18 (+0.33%) and Shanghai Composite at 4,110.81 (+0.11%); AP independently corroborated both rounded levels

text: Greater China markets stabilized on Wednesday, June 24. The Hang Seng rose 75.90 points (+0.33%) to 23,412.18, led by semiconductor and biotech constituents including Semicon Manufacturing Intl up 8.93%, WuXi AppTec up 8.33%, and Wuxi Biologics up 4.17%. The Shanghai Composite added 0.11% to 4,110.81. Both moves were modest stabilization bounces after the prior session's technology-led decline.

subject.name: Post-tech-selloff stabilization with AI valuation stress, oil declining, and dollar at one-year high

value.primary_driver: Stabilization after Tuesday global tech/semiconductor selloff; bargain hunting in battered markets (KOSPI +3.26%) versus continued pressure on Japanese equities
value.tech_selloff_context: Global semiconductor and AI-related shares fell Tuesday on concerns about stretched valuations and elevated US rate expectations; WSJ/S&P 500 fell 1.4% and Nasdaq fell 2.2% on Tuesday June 23
value.macro_support: Oil prices extended losses toward four-month lows as US-Iran negotiations progressed; Brent settled at $73.74 down 4.33%, AP reported Brent at $73.87 down 3.8% and US crude at $70.34 down 3.9%
value.dollar_strength: Dollar index rose 0.24% to 101.63, highest since May 2025; yen near 40-year lows at ~161.81
value.boj_context: BOJ summary released Wednesday showed some board members called for further rate hikes after the central bank raised rates to a 31-year high of 1.00%
value.risk_events_ahead: Micron Technology earnings after US close Wednesday; US PCE inflation data due Thursday (expected 4.1% YoY for May, highest in three years)

market_session: Asia-Pacific regular sessions and global macro context, Wednesday 2026-06-24
text: The Asia session on June 24 was a post-selloff stabilization after Tuesday's global technology rout. The KOSPI rebounded 3.26% on bargain hunting after plunging roughly 10% the prior day, while the Nikkei fell for a second day (-0.88%). Macro support came from further declines in oil prices (Brent settled down 4.33% at $73.74) as US-Iran negotiations progressed, but the dollar hit a one-year high (index at 101.63) and the yen traded near 40-year lows around 161.81. A BOJ summary showed some members pushing for further rate hikes after the 1.00% hike. Analysts cautioned about renewed volatility, with Micron earnings and US PCE data as the next risk events.
